title: Anglo-Saxon and Old English Vocabularies: selected vocabularies XIII–XX
summary: CME00034 is a University of Michigan electronic selection of vocabularies XIII–XX from volume I of Richard Paul Wülcker's revised 1884 edition of Thomas Wright's Anglo-Saxon and Old English Vocabularies. It is not one medieval work and not the complete printed volume. The selection brings together eight separately transmitted Latin–vernacular lexical texts from seven manuscripts, including two neighboring verse vocabularies in Harley MS 1002. This record distinguishes the repository XML, U-M encoding, Wright's 1857 collection, Wülcker's 1884 revised edition, manuscript witnesses, and digital facsimiles. Complete provider objects are cached where public terms permit; selected-only and request-only coverage is recorded explicitly.
review_notes: The U-M title's phrase 'Anglo-Saxon and Old English' describes the historical collection. The selected medieval vernacular matter is chiefly Middle English; Old English is not assigned merely from that title or its broad subject headings.
Printed locators are numbered columns, while Internet Archive and IIIF locators describe capture leaves or canvases. Those systems are kept separate.

Complete facsimile coverage means the complete sequence exposed by that provider at review time, including covers, inside covers, flyleaves, and blanks when exposed; it does not imply separate spine or edge photography or permission for unrestricted reuse.
The source XML's TEMPHEAD value '8-4-16' is retained as an inherited review notation, not normalized into a bibliographic date because its date order is unstated.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00034
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00034; the XML declares Middle English and Latin and encodes only vocabularies XIII–XX.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00034
entities.description: Public electronic text titled Anglo-Saxon and Old English vocabularies [extracts], encoding the 1884 title page and vocabularies XIII–XX.

entities.label: Wright and Wülcker, Anglo-Saxon and Old English Vocabularies, second edition, volume I (1884)
entities.description: Wülcker's revised and newly collated second edition of Wright's collection. CME00034 selects vocabularies XIII–XX, printed in columns 536–814, plus the edition title page.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Wright, Anglo-Saxon and Old English Vocabularies, 2nd ed., edited and collated by Richard Paul Wülcker, vol. 1: Vocabularies (London: Trübner & Co., 1884), cols. 536–814.
bibliographic.title: Anglo-Saxon and Old English Vocabularies

notes: The volume prints numbered columns rather than conventional page numbers. Vocabularies XIII–XX occupy capture leaves 302–446 in the complete Internet Archive scan inspected here.

entities.label: Thomas Wright, A Volume of Vocabularies (1857)
entities.description: Wright's first collection, retained and revised by Wülcker for seven of the eight selected numbered vocabularies. Wülcker's no. XV was a new addition to the second edition.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Wright, A Volume of Vocabularies: Illustrating the Condition and Manners of Our Forefathers, as Well as the History of the Forms of Elementary Education, and of the Languages Spoken in This Island from the Tenth Century to the Fifteenth (Liverpool, 1857).
bibliographic.title: A Volume of Vocabularies

entities.label: Worcester Cathedral Library MS F.174
entities.description: Fragmentary leaves of Ælfric's Grammar and Glossary, copied in the first half of the thirteenth century and recovered from a Worcester Cathedral binding; source witness for vocabulary XIII.

holding.notes: Current scholarly handlist; exact official institutional catalog record was not found.

notes: Sheets of varying size were pasted together and reused in a binding; text and leaf edges are fragmentary.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 978
entities.description: A composite miscellany probably associated with Reading Abbey; its trilingual list of herbs is the source for vocabulary XIV.

date_statements.label: Manuscript date
date_statements.value: third quarter of the thirteenth century, possibly 1261–1265

notes: Composite manuscript with multiple textual and linguistic components.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS O.5.4 (James 1285)
entities.description: A fifteenth-century grammatical and lexical codex. Its dictionary at modern fols. 96r–275r supplied vocabulary XV through a transcript by W. Aldis Wright.

holding.notes: James no. 1285; probable association with Battlefield College near Shrewsbury.

physical_description.extent: 4 + 294 folios in the catalog; digital labels extend through f. 295v
notes: Original white-skin binding over boards survives; clasps are lost.
The catalog warns that modern foliation is incorrect and adopts it for reference.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 1002
entities.description: A composite collection of grammatical texts. Fols. 113r–116r transmit the Distigius and Bursa Latini printed as vocabulary XVI; f. 116v transmits the body-parts vocabulary XVII.

notes: Complex foliation; post-1600 binding.

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 17 C XVII
entities.description: An early-fifteenth-century paper manuscript whose Latin–English nominalia at fols. 21r–27v supplied vocabulary XVIII.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 34276
entities.description: A fifteenth-century paper manuscript, formerly owned by Joseph Mayer. Its Nominale at fols. 12r–22r supplied vocabulary XIX.

notes: The 1857/1884 edition describes a then-portable roll-up vellum cover and a missing leaf; that historical description is not treated as a current binding statement.

entities.label: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library MS 594
entities.description: A fifteenth-century illustrated Latin–English vocabulary at fols. 1r–22v, with numerous marginal drawings, printed as vocabulary XX. F. 23 contains adjacent notes and letter formulae outside the selected work.

holding.notes: Formerly owned by Lord Londesborough; later Aldenham and Norman Pearson MS 273; bequeathed to Yale in 1976.

notes: Folding format; paper is badly damaged and repaired on stubs.
Nineteenth-century blind-tooled brown calf binding over boards with a gold title.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Wright and Wülcker 1884, volume I
entities.description: Complete public scan of the 1884 volume from the University of Toronto copy. The BookReader sequence exposes front and back covers, inside surfaces and blanks, but no separate spine or edge views.
entities.reproduction_format: 452 capture leaves in BookReader metadata; 450-page access PDF

entities.label: Internet Archive incomplete scan record anglosaxonoldeng01wrig
entities.description: A second Internet Archive item examined and rejected as the principal surrogate because its scan sequence jumps from printed page 319 to 625 and therefore omits much of CME00034.
entities.reproduction_format: Incomplete digital scan; local metadata and scandata retained as negative-selection evidence

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge complete IIIF sequence for MS O.5.4
entities.description: The complete 604-canvas sequence exposed by Trinity: front cover, inside-front, four front flyleaves, fols. 1r–295v, four back-flyleaf surfaces, inside-back, and back cover. No separate spine or edge canvases are exposed.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest and 1600-pixel JPEG derivatives

entities.label: Yale selected digital image of Beinecke MS 594, fols. 3v–4r
entities.description: One publicly downloadable spread, image identifier 1312757. It is complete for that published one-image selection but partial for the work at fols. 1r–22v and for the physical manuscript.

agents.name: Thomas Wright
notes: Compiler, original editor, transcriber, and commentator of the 1857 collection; not the medieval author of the selected vocabularies.

agents.name: Richard Paul Wülcker
notes: Editor, compiler, reviser, collator, and commentator of the 1884 second edition.

agents.name: William Aldis Wright
notes: Supplied the manuscript transcript for vocabulary XV; not the compiler of its medieval dictionary.

agents.name: Stanford Center for Medieval and Early Modern Studies
The Master and Fellows of Trinity College Cambridge
Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University

rights.component: Encoded textual content represented by the repository XML
rights.basis: The U-M item identifies the materials as public domain and displays No Copyright–United States; the medieval texts and 1884 edition are also out of copyright in the United States.
notes: This concerns encoded textual content, not third-party manuscript photography or platform infrastructure.

rights.component: U-M electronic text and underlying textual content
rights.basis: U-M states that the materials are public domain and links No Copyright–United States, while requesting use for educational and research purposes.

rights.component: Internet Archive access files and scan photography
rights.basis: The item is publicly downloadable and its underlying 1884 printed content is public domain in the United States, but no exact item-level license for the scan files was found in the reviewed metadata.
notes: The cached PDF is a private research copy; public-domain status of the text is not treated as an automatic license for every digital file component.

rights.basis: The official catalog and IIIF manifest attribute copyright to the Master and Fellows of Trinity College Cambridge and license the digital images under Creative Commons Attribution–NonCommercial 4.0 International.
notes: Private caching does not broaden the license; redistribution must remain noncommercial, attributed, and compliant with CC BY-NC 4.0.

rights.basis: Beinecke permits free download of existing Digital Collections images but states that supplying an image does not itself authorize publication and that users must determine copyright status. No image-specific license was found.
notes: The fifteenth-century manuscript content is public domain, but reuse of the provider photograph is assessed separately; the cached JPEG is for private research.

rights.component: Worcester Cathedral manuscript photographs and microfilms
rights.basis: The Cathedral's reproduction page requires permission for study and publication and states that the Dean and Chapter retain copyright and publication rights in supplied photographs.

rights.component: Any British Library manuscript reproductions ordered for the four BL witnesses
rights.basis: No item-specific manuscript-image license was found. Any supplied reproduction and reuse would be governed by the terms communicated with the order.
notes: This rights assessment is referenced from Harley MS 978 as a representative access record and applies equally cautiously to requested images of Harley MS 1002, Royal MS 17 C XVII, and Add MS 34276.

editorial_practices.description: Wright selected manuscript vocabularies to illustrate language, education, and material culture, sought to reproduce their wording including errors, collated multiple copies when available, and annotated selectively.
editorial_practices.scope: Wright's 1857 collection, including the seven components later retained in CME00034

editorial_practices.source_feature: Wright's first-edition texts and accessible manuscripts
editorial_practices.representation: Revised readings, new collation, and additions marked R.W.
editorial_practices.description: Wülcker retained Wright's explanations, removed and added articles, compared retained articles anew with accessible manuscripts, and marked his own additions with the initials R.W.; no. XV was newly supplied from a W. Aldis Wright transcript.
editorial_practices.scope: The 1884 second edition and specifically selected vocabularies XIII–XX

editorial_practices.representation: Encoded title page and vocabularies XIII–XX only
editorial_practices.description: The U-M item omits the earlier vocabularies, preliminaries beyond the title page, and later volume matter, while preserving the complete printed run of the eight selected numbered vocabularies.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A larger Latin dictionary at Trinity O.5.4, modern ff. 96r–275r
editorial_practices.representation: Latin entries with selected English equivalents and later additions
editorial_practices.description: Vocabulary XV is a lexical extraction from a much larger dictionary span, not a continuous diplomatic edition of every manuscript entry in those folios.

evidence.citation: General Corpus CME source XML for CME00034

evidence.summary: Identifies the repository artifact and preserves the eight numbered vocabulary components, edition title page, printed-column/page markers, notes, figures, and structure.

evidence.citation: Local structural and checksum inspection of CME00034.xml
evidence.locator: Element counts and component boundaries
evidence.summary: Confirmed two DIV1 elements (title page and collection), eight numbered DIV2 vocabulary components, PB range 302–446, 9,548 textual ITEM elements plus one administrative TEMPHEAD ITEM, 376 L elements, two LG elements, 1,006 MILESTONE elements, 595 NOTE elements, and the exact repository hash/blob identity.

evidence.summary: States that the materials are public domain, gives educational/research guidance, and displays No Copyright–United States.

evidence.citation: Wright and Wülcker, Anglo-Saxon and Old English Vocabularies, 2nd ed., vol. I (1884)
evidence.locator: title page; contents; cols. 536–814
evidence.summary: Establishes the full title, responsibility, edition, volume, imprint, eight selected vocabulary headings, and printed-column boundaries.

evidence.citation: Wülcker's second-edition preface and Wright's reprinted 1857 preface
evidence.locator: prefaces in volume I
evidence.summary: Explains the 1857/1884 revision history, manuscript re-collation, Wülcker's additions and removals, W. Aldis Wright's transcript for no. XV, the R.W. notation, Wright's chronological and transcriptional aims, and patron/provenance context.

evidence.citation: Editorial introductions and texts for vocabularies XIII–XX in Wright and Wülcker 1884
evidence.summary: Names and describes the seven manuscript sources, assigns historical dates and dialect observations, identifies component forms, and documents fragmentary or illustrated features and lacunae.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of cached complete 1884 volume PDF
evidence.locator: PDF/capture leaves 5, 302–446 and outer object boundaries
evidence.summary: Verified the title page, selected range, start and end leaves, printed columns 536–814, and complete-volume capture coverage from front through back cover.

evidence.locator: metadata and file list
evidence.summary: Identifies the 1884 University of Toronto item, scan year, ARK, access PDF, and provider file checksums.

evidence.summary: Records 452 capture leaves, leaf 1 front cover, leaf 450 back cover, title leaf 5, work leaves 302–446, and two excluded color-card leaves.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for anglosaxonoldeng01wrig

evidence.citation: The Production and Use of English Manuscripts 1060 to 1220, Worcester Cathedral Library F.174
evidence.locator: date, contents, origin, provenance, physical description
evidence.summary: Identifies the surviving Ælfric Grammar/Glossary leaves as s. xiii1, of Worcester provenance, formerly reused in a binding, with 66 surviving leaves and incomplete text.

evidence.citation: Worcester Cathedral Library, Lost Manuscripts Discovered by Sir Thomas Phillipps
evidence.summary: Confirms Phillipps's discovery of binding leaves now bound as MS F.174 and identifies the Cathedral's copyright in the article images.

evidence.citation: Worcester Cathedral Library access page
evidence.summary: Provides the institutional research-access route; no complete public facsimile is linked.

evidence.citation: Worcester Cathedral Library photographs and microfilms policy
evidence.summary: Requires contact and permission forms for supplied images, describes fees, and reserves copyright/publication control in Cathedral photography.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 978
evidence.locator: parent description, date, languages, origin/provenance, physical description, access
evidence.summary: Describes the thirteenth-century composite miscellany, probable Reading Abbey association, material extent, languages, and current absence of linked digital content.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 978 medical miscellany
evidence.locator: ff. 22r–37r; list of herbs ff. 26r–27v
evidence.summary: Locates the trilingual Chaudes herbes list at current ff. 26r–27v and supplies its incipit and explicit, securely matching vocabulary XIV.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge, MS O.5.4 catalog record
evidence.locator: James 1285; contents no. 21; ff. 96–275; physical description; provenance; rights
evidence.summary: Identifies the fifteenth-century dictionary span printed by Wülcker from W. Aldis Wright's copy, records probable Battlefield provenance, warns about modern foliation, and describes the binding and codex.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge IIIF manifest for MS O.5.4
evidence.locator: 604 canvases; sequence positions 1–604 and 199–557
evidence.summary: Exposes the complete provider sequence from front cover through back cover and maps the vocabulary dictionary span to modern ff. 96r–275r at positions 199–557.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge manuscript catalog and IIIF rights statement
evidence.locator: copyright and license statement
evidence.summary: Attributes image copyright to the Master and Fellows and licenses the images under CC BY-NC 4.0.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 1002
evidence.locator: date, physical description, contents at ff. 113r–116v, digital-content status
evidence.summary: Locates Distigius at f. 113r–v, Bursa Latini at ff. 114r–116r, and Os facies mentum at f. 116v, matching vocabularies XVI and XVII; no digital content is linked.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Royal MS 17 C XVII
evidence.locator: date, language, physical description, item 4a at f. 21
evidence.summary: Describes the 1400–1440 manuscript and explicitly identifies the Nomina membrorum hominis nominalia beginning f. 21 as printed by Wright and Wülcker; the next item boundaries support ff. 21r–27v.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Add MS 34276
evidence.locator: physical description; item 2 from f. 12; following item at f. 22b; provenance
evidence.summary: Describes the fifteenth-century paper manuscript, Joseph Mayer ownership, and the Nominale beginning f. 12 that Wright printed; the following item bounds it near f. 22.

evidence.citation: Sara M. Pons-Sanz, discussion of the Mayer Nominale in Add MS 34276
evidence.locator: Add MS 34276, fols. 12–22; Wülcker no. XIX, cols. 673–744
evidence.summary: Confirms the manuscript, folio span, edition cross-reference, fifteenth-century date, and Lincolnshire linguistic association for the Mayer Nominale.

evidence.citation: Yale Beinecke Pre-1600 Manuscripts, MS 594
evidence.locator: ff. 1r–22v, date, physical description, binding, provenance, bibliography
evidence.summary: Describes the fifteenth-century illustrated Latin–English vocabulary, its complete folio span, adjacent f. 23, physical state, binding, provenance, and direct Wright/Wülcker edition cross-reference.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of cached Yale image 1312757

evidence.citation: Beinecke Library reproduction orders
evidence.summary: States that existing Digital Collections images may be downloaded free and that new images can be requested, while warning that image provision does not authorize publication.

evidence.citation: Beinecke Library copyright questions
evidence.summary: Explains that Yale often does not hold copyright, users must determine status, public-domain material does not require permission, citation is expected, and provider terms apply.

evidence.citation: British Library image-order and reproduction route

open_questions.question: Which current folios of Worcester Cathedral Library F.174 correspond exactly to vocabulary XIII, and can a complete object-level facsimile be obtained?
next_steps: Ask Worcester Cathedral Library to reconcile Wülcker's text with current foliation.
Request all surviving leaves and complete binding/object views under the Cathedral's terms.

open_questions.question: How does Wülcker's historical locator Harley 978 f. 24v map to the current catalog's ff. 26r–27v for the herb glossary?
next_steps: Ask the British Library for the foliation history or inspect a complete reproduction with both modern and historical numeration visible.

open_questions.question: Can complete object-level facsimiles be obtained for Harley MSS 978 and 1002, Royal MS 17 C XVII, and Add MS 34276?
next_steps: Submit scoped BL reproduction enquiries for every leaf and binding surface of each object.
Record the supplied files, exact physical boundaries, fees, and reuse terms if obtained.

open_questions.question: Does Beinecke hold an unpublished complete digital sequence for MS 594, including binding and f. 23, that can be supplied for research?
next_steps: Request all folios and binding surfaces through Beinecke reproduction services.
Ask whether an authenticated IIIF Presentation manifest or existing master-image inventory can be provided and what reuse terms apply.

open_questions.question: Which historical editorial dates and dialect regions for the eight vocabularies remain accepted in current lexical scholarship?
next_steps: Check current specialist editions or linguistic studies component by component, keeping text composition, witness copying, dialect, and provenance separate.
